query: 2026 Cuban crisis: current state of US-Cuba negotiations and tensions as of July 2026. What high-level US official visits to Cuba have occurred or are planned in summer 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of my findings on the 2026 Cuban crisis and US-Cuba negotiations as of July 2026:

**On high-level official visits (the core question):**
- The only confirmed high-level US visit was a **senior US State Department delegation** that flew to Havana on a US government aircraft in **mid-April 2026** — described as "one of the highest-level visits of US officials in roughly a decade" and the first US government plane to land in Cuba (outside Guantánamo) since Obama's 2016 visit.
- **No high-level US official visit is publicly confirmed or scheduled for summer 2026.** Engagement has shifted away from in-person visits toward escalating sanctions and a UN confrontation.

**On the state of negotiations/tensions:**
- Talks (secret, reportedly initiated by Raúl Castro, publicly confirmed March 13) are **stalled**. Cuba's FM said "no progress" (June 30); Rubio said a deal is "not high" in likelihood (May 21).
- US demands reportedly include Díaz-Canel's resignation, which Cuba refused.
- The US has escalated: fuel blockade (Feb), arrest warrant for Raúl Castro (May), sanctions on Díaz-Canel/family (June) and state oil firm Cupet (June 11), plus military-action threats.
- A humanitarian crisis is deepening (infant mortality doubled to ~9.9/1,000, food production down 60%, ran out of oil/diesel May 14).
- **July 7 UN showdown:** The US failed to block a Cuba-requested extraordinary UN debate on the embargo (vote 136–9, 30 abstentions).

All findings are documented with sources and quantitative data points in the structured output.

key_facts: Only confirmed high-level US visit: a senior US State Department delegation flew to Havana on a US government aircraft in mid-April 2026 — 'one of the highest-level visits of US officials in roughly a decade' and the first US government plane to land in Cuba (outside Guantanamo) since Obama's 2016 visit.
No high-level US official visit to Cuba is publicly confirmed or scheduled for summer 2026 as of early July 2026.
Negotiations are stalled: Cuban FM Bruno Rodriguez said on June 30, 2026 that talks show 'no progress'; Sec. Rubio said on May 21, 2026 a peaceful deal is 'not high' in likelihood.
US reportedly demands resignation of President Miguel Diaz-Canel; Cuba publicly refused.
Talks were secret/historic, publicly confirmed by Diaz-Canel on March 13, 2026; a July 6, 2026 report says they were initiated by Raul Castro.
US began blocking oil tankers to Cuba in February 2026 — first effective US blockade since the 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis.
Cuba released 51 political prisoners (March) then 2,000+ prisoners (April 3, 2026) amid negotiations.
US escalation: arrest warrant for Raul Castro (May); sanctions on Diaz-Canel and family (early June); sanctions on state oil firm Cupet (June 11); military-action threats raised (May 22).
Humanitarian crisis by June 2026 (OHCHR): infant mortality ~9.9/1,000 (roughly doubled), childhood cancer survival 65%, food production down 60%, medicines at 30% of normal.
May 14, 2026: Cuba's Energy Ministry warned the country had run out of oil and diesel; blackouts up to ~40 hours.

July 7, 2026: UN General Assembly voted 136-9 with 30 abstentions to open an extraordinary debate on the US embargo, defeating a US ('Rubio Cable') campaign to block it; abstentions more than doubled vs. October 2025.
Crisis stems from the 2026 US intervention in Venezuela that ousted Maduro and cut Venezuelan oil to Cuba; US goal reportedly regime change by end of 2026.
